title: Assembly Language Guide
description: Complete guide to writing assembly programs in DemiEngine with test directives

sections.title: Assembly Language Overview
sections.content: DemiEngine includes a powerful assembly language that supports human-readable mnemonics, labels, and in-assembly testing. The assembler converts `.asm` files into executable bytecode.

sections.title: Basic Syntax
sections.content:
**Mnemonics:** Human-readable instruction names (e.g., `MOV`, `ADD`, `JMP`)

**Registers:** R0-R7 for basic operations, R0-R15 for extended

**Immediates:** Numeric values (`10`, `0x0A`, `0b1010`)

**Labels:** Named addresses for jumps and calls

**Comments:** Lines starting with `;` or `#`

sections.title: Hello World Example
sections.content:
```asm
; Hello World in DemiEngine Assembly

LOAD_IMM R0, message_addr
OUTSTR 1, R0
HALT

message_addr:
    DB "Hello, World!", 0
```

sections.title: Instruction Format
sections.content:
**Load Immediate:**
```asm
LOAD_IMM R0, 42        ; Load 42 into R0
LOAD_IMM R1, 0xFF      ; Load 255 into R1
```

**Arithmetic:**
```asm
ADD R0, R1             ; R0 = R0 + R1
SUB R2, R3             ; R2 = R2 - R3
MUL R0, R1             ; R0 = R0 * R1
DIV R0, R1             ; R0 = R0 / R1
INC R0                 ; R0++
DEC R1                 ; R1--
```

**Memory:**
```asm
LOAD R0, [100]         ; Load from address 100
STORE [100], R0        ; Store R0 to address 100
```

**Control Flow:**
```asm
JMP label              ; Unconditional jump
CMP R0, R1             ; Compare registers
JZ label               ; Jump if zero
JNZ label              ; Jump if not zero
CALL function          ; Call subroutine
RET                    ; Return from subroutine
```

sections.title: Labels and Jumps
sections.content:
```asm
; Loop example with labels
LOAD_IMM R0, 10        ; Counter
LOAD_IMM R1, 0         ; Comparison value

loop_start:
    DEC R0
    CMP R0, R1
    JNZ loop_start     ; Continue if not zero

HALT
```

sections.title: Data Directives
sections.content:
**Define Byte (DB):**
```asm
message:  DB "Hello", 0
bytes:    DB 0x10, 0x20, 0x30
array:    DB 1, 2, 3, 4, 5
```

**Origin (.org):**
```asm
.org 0x100             ; Set code origin to 0x100
```

sections.title: In-Assembly Testing
sections.content:
DemiEngine supports powerful in-assembly testing with `#test` blocks:

```asm
#test addition_test
    #description Tests basic addition
    #author DemiEngine Team
    #category Arithmetic
    #tag basic, math
    
    LOAD_IMM R0, 5
    LOAD_IMM R1, 3
    ADD R0, R1
    
    #assert_eq R0, 8
#endtest
```

sections.title: Test Directives
sections.content:
**Test Metadata:**
- `#description` - Brief test description
- `#author` - Test author/team
- `#category` - Test category
- `#tag` - Comma-separated tags

**Assertions:**
- `#assert_eq <reg>, <value>` - Assert register equals value
- `#assert_ne <reg>, <value>` - Assert not equal
- `#assert_lt <reg>, <value>` - Assert less than
- `#assert_gt <reg>, <value>` - Assert greater than
- `#assert_le <reg>, <value>` - Assert less or equal
- `#assert_ge <reg>, <value>` - Assert greater or equal
- `#assert_mem <addr>, <value>` - Assert memory value
- `#assert_flag <flag>` - Assert flag is set
- `#expect_error` - Expect test to trigger error

sections.title: Complete Test Example
sections.content:
```asm
#test stack_operations
    #description Tests PUSH and POP maintain LIFO order
    #author DemiEngine Team
    #category Stack
    #tag stack, lifo, order
    
    ; Push three values
    LOAD_IMM R0, 10
    PUSH R0
    LOAD_IMM R0, 20
    PUSH R0
    LOAD_IMM R0, 30
    PUSH R0
    
    ; Pop and verify LIFO order
    POP R1
    #assert_eq R1, 30
    
    POP R2
    #assert_eq R2, 20
    
    POP R3
    #assert_eq R3, 10
#endtest
```

sections.title: Running Tests
sections.content:
```bash
# Run all assembly tests
./bin/demi-engine -at

# Show only failures
./bin/demi-engine -at --show=fails

# Show only successes
./bin/demi-engine -at --show=success

# Test specific file
./bin/demi-engine -at tests/asm/my_test.asm

# Quiet mode (minimal output)
./bin/demi-engine -atq
```

sections.title: Function Call Example
sections.content:
```asm
; Main program
LOAD_IMM R0, 5
CALL square_function
; R0 now contains 25
HALT

; Function: square a number
square_function:
    MOV R1, R0         ; Copy input
    MUL R0, R1         ; R0 = R0 * R1
    RET
```

sections.title: Loop Example
sections.content:
```asm
; Sum numbers from 1 to 10
LOAD_IMM R0, 10        ; Counter
LOAD_IMM R1, 0         ; Sum accumulator
LOAD_IMM R2, 0         ; Comparison value

loop:
    ADD R1, R0         ; Add counter to sum
    DEC R0             ; Decrement counter
    CMP R0, R2         ; Compare to zero
    JNZ loop           ; Continue if not zero

; R1 now contains 55 (sum of 1-10)
HALT
```

sections.title: String I/O Example
sections.content:
```asm
; Print multiple strings
LOAD_IMM R0, msg1
OUTSTR 1, R0

LOAD_IMM R0, msg2
OUTSTR 1, R0

HALT

msg1: DB "Hello, ", 0
msg2: DB "World!", 0
```

sections.title: Best Practices
sections.content:
1. **Use meaningful labels** - `calculate_sum` instead of `label1`
2. **Comment complex logic** - Explain what the code does
3. **Organize code in sections** - Separate code, data, and functions
4. **Test edge cases** - Zero values, maximum values, negative results
5. **Use consistent naming** - snake_case for labels, UPPERCASE for constants
6. **Document register usage** - Comment which registers hold what data
7. **Validate inputs** - Check for division by zero, invalid addresses
